events.text: Patient is a 64-year-old male of South Asian (Indian) ethnicity, non-Hispanic, BMI 23.6, non-smoker, with a history of well-controlled type 2 diabetes on metformin and a family history of colorectal cancer in his father.
At age 63, patient was found to have elevated CEA (42 ng/mL) on routine bloodwork; subsequent colonoscopy revealed a 5 cm mass in the sigmoid colon (C18.7).
Sigmoid colon biopsy demonstrated moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma (histology 8140/3) with focal cribriform architecture and tumor budding (high grade).

Molecular profiling identified KRAS G12D mutation (allele frequency 34%), BRAF wild-type, PIK3CA wild-type, TP53 R248W mutation, APC Q1367* truncation; microsatellite stable (MSS) by next-generation sequencing; no NTRK or RET fusions; HER2 non-amplified.

CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at age 63 showed sigmoid colon mass with adjacent lymphadenopathy and two hepatic metastases in segment VI (2.4 cm) and segment III (1.7 cm); scattered subcentimeter pulmonary nodules likely benign; staged T3N1aM1a.
At age 63, CA 19-9 was 68 U/mL; left-sided primary with potentially resectable oligometastatic liver disease; tumor board recommended neoadjuvant systemic therapy with planned surgical consolidation.
At age 63, patient initiated FOLFOX plus bevacizumab every 2 weeks as first-line therapy.
After cycle 4 of FOLFOX, patient developed grade 2 cold-triggered peripheral neuropathy in hands with moderate interference with daily activities; also noted grade 1 thrombocytopenia.

Restaging CT after 8 cycles of FOLFOX-bevacizumab showed excellent partial response; sigmoid mass notably reduced, hepatic metastases decreased (segment VI now 1.1 cm, segment III now 0.7 cm); CEA dropped to 7.6 ng/mL.
At age 64, CA 19-9 normalized to 22 U/mL; liver surgeon reviewed imaging and deemed lesions resectable; planned simultaneous approach.
At age 64, patient underwent laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy, segment VI wedge resection, and segment III wedge resection in a single operative session.
Sigmoid colectomy specimen showed residual moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma, ypT2N0 (0 of 21 lymph nodes positive), extensive treatment effect with 25% viable tumor, negative margins.

Liver resection specimens showed treated metastatic adenocarcinoma with 15% viable tumor in segment VI lesion and near-complete response in segment III lesion (less than 5% viable); all margins negative (R0).
At age 64, resumed postoperative FOLFOX for 4 additional cycles (12 total perioperative); bevacizumab held for 8 weeks postoperatively per surgical protocol.

At age 64, cumulative oxaliplatin neuropathy progressed to grade 2 with persistent numbness in hands and feet and difficulty with fine motor tasks; completed planned 12 cycles but with last 2 at 75% oxaliplatin dose.
At age 64, patient developed grade 1 hepatic steatosis noted on surveillance imaging, attributed to combination of chemotherapy and diabetes-related fatty liver.
Surveillance CT at age 64 (3 months after completing chemotherapy) showed postoperative changes without evidence of recurrent disease; CEA 3.4 ng/mL.
CT at age 64 (6 months post-surgery) remained without evidence of disease; CEA 2.9 ng/mL; CA 19-9 was 16 U/mL.

At age 64, patient entered active surveillance with CEA every 3 months, CT every 3-6 months, and colonoscopy scheduled at 1 year; neuropathy slowly improving; glycemic control maintained on metformin; ECOG 0.
Surveillance colonoscopy at age 64 showed clean anastomosis without recurrence; two diminutive tubular adenomas removed from the transverse colon.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
Sigmoid colon biopsy demonstrated moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma (histology 8140/3) with focal cribriform architecture and tumor budding (high grade).

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
Molecular profiling identified KRAS G12D mutation (allele frequency 34%), BRAF wild-type, PIK3CA wild-type, TP53 R248W mutation, APC Q1367* truncation; microsatellite stable (MSS) by next-generation sequencing; no NTRK or RET fusions; HER2 non-amplified.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at age 63 showed sigmoid colon mass with adjacent lymphadenopathy and two hepatic metastases in segment VI (2.4 cm) and segment III (1.7 cm); scattered subcentimeter pulmonary nodules likely benign; staged T3N1aM1a.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 63, CA 19-9 was 68 U/mL; left-sided primary with potentially resectable oligometastatic liver disease; tumor board recommended neoadjuvant systemic therapy with planned surgical consolidation.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
Restaging CT after 8 cycles of FOLFOX-bevacizumab showed excellent partial response; sigmoid mass notably reduced, hepatic metastases decreased (segment VI now 1.1 cm, segment III now 0.7 cm); CEA dropped to 7.6 ng/mL.

Please correlate clinically.

scenario_blurb: Stage IV colorectal cancer with KRAS G12D mutation. Left-sided and right-sided primary tumors. Treated with FOLFOX or FOLFIRI plus bevacizumab. Some with liver-limited metastases considered for hepatic resection. MSI/MMR testing performed.
